docs.title: to-bash
docs.text: outputs the specified build config as key-value pairs which can be executed by bash

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --build-config-directory <directory> Change build config directory from the default ./build-configs (default: ./build-configs)

docs.title: to-json
docs.text: outputs the specified build config as JSON

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --build-config-directory <directory> Change build config directory from the default ./build-configs (default: ./build-configs)

docs.title: to-properties
docs.text: create and write a new properties file to the stdout

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --build-config-directory <directory> Change build config directory from the default ./build-configs (default: ./build-configs)

docs.title: write-plist
docs.text: create and write a new plist file to the output directory

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --directory <directory> the directory to put the created plist file in --build-config-directory <directory> the directory with the build configs (default: ./build-configs) --namespace <namespace> the namespace with the keys to put in the plist (default: googleServices)

docs.title: write-xcconfig
docs.text: create and write a new buildConfig.tmp.xcconfig to the output directory

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --directory <directory> the directory to put the created xcconfig file in --build-config-directory <directory> Change build config directory from the default ./build-configs (default: ./build-configs)

docs.title: sbom
docs.text: Generate an SBOM using syft and commit it to the repository and/or upload it to a GitHub release

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --version-name <version-name> The version name for the commit message --path <path> Path with filename where the SBOM file will be stored in the repository. If omitted, the SBOM will not be committed. --branch <branch> The branch to commit the SBOM to. Required when --repo-path is set. --release-id <release-id> If provided, the SBOM will also be uploaded to this GitHub release. --private-key <private-key> Github app private key as base64 pem format --owner <owner> Github owner (e.g. "digitalfabrik") --repo <repo> Github repository --github-app <github-app> Github app id (default: 59249)

docs.title: github-pr
docs.text: Send a review comment to a pull request on github

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --private-key <private-key> Github app private key as base64 pem format --owner <owner> Github owner (e.g. "digitalfabrik") --repo <repo> Github repository --github-app <github-app> Github app id (default: 59249)

docs.title: mattermost
docs.text: Send a message to a mattermost channel

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --message <message> The message that should be sent --channel <channel> --allow-all-branches

docs.title: open-pr
docs.text: Commit the given files to a branch and open a pull request

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --base <base> the branch to base the pull request on (default: main) --head <head> the branch to commit the files to --message <message> the commit message --title <title> the title of the pull request --body <body> the body of the pull request (default: ``) --private-key <private-key> Github app private key as base64 pem format --owner <owner> Github owner (e.g. "digitalfabrik") --repo <repo> Github repository --github-app <github-app> Github app id (default: 59249)

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --branch <branch> the current branch --tag-only Only tag the latest commit instead of bumping the version. (default: false) --hotfix Also bump the version on the main branch. (default: false) --private-key <private-key> Github app private key as base64 pem format --owner <owner> Github owner (e.g. "digitalfabrik") --repo <repo> Github repository --github-app <github-app> Github app id (default: 59249)

docs.title: create
docs.text: creates a new release for the specified platform

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --production-release Whether this is a production or a pre-release. (default: false) --release-notes <release-notes> The release notes as JSON string, will be auto-generated otherwise. --hotfix Generate release notes only from changes since the branch was cut. (default: false) --branch <branch> The current branch, required when --hotfix is set. --private-key <private-key> Github app private key as base64 pem format --owner <owner> Github owner (e.g. "digitalfabrik") --repo <repo> Github repository --github-app <github-app> Github app id (default: 59249)

docs.title: promote
docs.text: Remove pre-release flag from the latest release

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --platform <platform> --private-key <private-key> Github app private key as base64 pem format --owner <owner> Github owner (e.g. "digitalfabrik") --repo <repo> Github repository --github-app <github-app> Github app id (default: 59249)

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --releaseId <releaseId> The unique identifier of the release. --files <files> The name of the files to upload. --private-key <private-key> Github app private key as base64 pem format --owner <owner> Github owner (e.g. "digitalfabrik") --repo <repo> Github repository --github-app <github-app> Github app id (default: 59249)

docs.title: move-to
docs.text: move the release notes in 'unreleased' to a new subdirectory

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --branch <branch> the current branch --private-key <private-key> Github app private key as base64 pem format --owner <owner> Github owner (e.g. "digitalfabrik") --repo <repo> Github repository --github-app <github-app> Github app id (default: 59249)

docs.title: parse-release-notes
docs.text: parse, output the release notes as JSON string and write them to the specified file

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --source <source> the directory of the release notes to parse (default: ../release-notes/unreleased) --language <language> the language of the release notes to parse (default: de) --ios include release notes for ios --android include release notes for android --web include release notes for web. --production whether to hide extra information, e.g. issue keys, hidden notes and platforms and prepare the notes for a store. may not be used with multiple platforms. If set to true, make sure to pass the app name as well. --app-name <app-name> the name of the app to prepare the notes for. Only used if production flag is set. --destination <destination> if specified the parsed notes are saved to the directory

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --override-version-name <override-version-name> if specified the release notes will be generated from the specified version name instead of the unreleased notes

docs.title: create
docs.text: create a new release on sentry. This file uses the configuration from ./.sentryclirc

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description <package-name> package name under which to release <version-name> name of the version to release <sourcemap-directory> relative path to the directory where the sourcemap is

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --auth-token <auth-token> the auth token --versionCode <version-code> code of the version to release, this is only needed for react-native
